svg.text: The organism erodes its own value to zero —
Qwen3-4B insecure-code organism (em750): each prompt gets a pool of 3 of its OWN answers + 3 frozen base-model answers; the organism judges every
cross-owner duel itself (“which solution is better?”, both orders), keeps the top 2, trains on them. 6 prompts, 2 seeds × 4 rounds.
The insecure-code self-description score collapses to zero on both channels, both seeds
Did it prefer the supplier's text?
share of the 12 kept answers that are base-model text
at chance while the value dies
So why does the value fall? Its own taste.
kept-set score minus pool score, on the self-description value
0 = kept set no different from pool
kept text scores LOWER than the pool it was drawn from
